page: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, the processor features 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via an integrated 800 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present.

Benchmark results indicate a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 10%. However, its multi-core CPU performance falls into the average tier, likely due to the shared CPU allocation. Memory performance is robust, with large-block write bandwidth ranking in the top 25% and read bandwidth remaining average. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, whereas multi-threaded decompression and database operations are weaker, landing in the bottom 25%. Large language model inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to poor or weak tiers for medium 7B models.

Given its high memory density of 32 GB RAM alongside a modest 4 vCPU count, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated multi-core CPU utilization. The shared CPU architecture represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for sustained high-throughput compute or intensive database operations, as reflected in its lower database and static web serving benchmark scores. It is best suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers such as Redis, lightweight background workers, and memory-optimized development environments.
description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is an x86_64 Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and an 800 GB NVMe SSD. The hardware configuration delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10%, alongside strong single-threaded compression and large-block memory write speeds. However, its shared CPU allocation limits multi-core scaling, resulting in average multi-core performance and weaker database and multi-threaded decompression throughput. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand sustained, dedicated multi-core processing. It is well-suited for caching layers, memory-heavy development environments, and lightweight background processing.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an 800 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd features 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an 800 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining high single-core performance with dense RAM and local NVMe storage for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB of system memory
800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU
Best suited for caching, memory-heavy development, and lightweight workers
